summary: Incendiary late‑'69 set with blazing blues and tight acoustic opener
blurb: The show opens with an unexpected acoustic quartet featuring "Monkey And The Engineer", "Little Sadie" and "Long Black Limousine" before launching into a high‑energy electric second set highlighted by "Cumberland Blues", "Hard To Handle" and "Black Peter". Jerry’s humor shines in the Wake Up Little Susie tease, and the band stays on fire throughout. Recording quality is solid across soundboard sources, making this an essential early Dead performance.
review: December 19, 1969 at the Fillmore Auditorium captures a Grateful Dead that were literally "on fire". A rare four‑song acoustic opener – "Monkey And The Engineer", "Little Sadie", "Long Black Limousine" and "I've Been All Around This World" – sees Jerry and Bob trading leads while Phil’s traffic delay forces an intimate start. Once the full band rolls in, the second set erupts with classic blues‑rock fuel: a ripping "Cumberland Blues", soulful "Black Peter", swaggering "Hard To Handle" and a rousing "Good Lovin'" that keep the momentum rolling into the extended jam sequence ending with "Turn On Your Lovelight". Across all three recordings reviewers note the band’s tight, high‑energy performance and the novelty of hearing the first live rendition of "Mason's Children". Soundboard captures are clean and vivid, especially the Clugston SBD that delivers clear instrument separation without distracting hiss, making this show a must‑listen snapshot of the Dead’s evolving live prowess.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings carry positive sentiment, consistently praising the energetic performance, standout blues numbers and the unique acoustic opening. No negative comments about playing are present, so a strong positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Solid soundboard capture with clear audio that showcases the band’s incendiary late‑year energy and tight set flow.

key_highlights: Rare acoustic quartet opening sets a unique tone for the night.
"Cumberland Blues", "Hard To Handle" and "Black Peter" deliver blistering blues power.
First live performance of "Mason's Children" adds historic value.
High‑quality soundboard recording preserves the band’s tight, high‑energy playing.
song_highlights: I've Been All Around This World

band_performance.Jerry: Shows humor with the Wake Up Little Susie tease and contributes strong acoustic guitar work; his lead throughout the electric set is tight and fiery.
band_performance.Phil: Arrives late, missing the acoustic opener, but provides solid bass foundation once onstage; no criticism noted.
band_performance.Bob: Participates enthusiastically in the acoustic quartet and backs the electric set with reliable rhythm guitar.

must_listen_sequences: I've Been All Around This World
